mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 0.25% signal an emergency monetary easing regime that reduces equity discount rates and increases asset price multiples. This mechanism operates continuously whenever rates are at or near zero, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% as of 2020-07-01 indicates emergency easing
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 45% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window in zero-rate regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 9.5% signals severe labor market deterioration that depresses consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as market reprices growth downward.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 10.2% as of 2020-07-01 indicates post-shock labor market stress
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE > 9.5%, or correlation between UNRATE changes and 5-day forward returns is positive (r > 0.05) over any 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month by more than 2% signals weakening consumer demand and forward earnings compression. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as equity valuations contract.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 535,982M as of 2020-07-01 reflects post-shock demand recovery
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite RSAFS decline > 2%, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly decline, signaling simultaneous supply-side and labor-market weakness. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices structural growth downward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 1% and PAYEMS declines > 0.5% month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 100.17 and PAYEMS at 139,582K as of 2020-07-01 show post-shock recovery
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when both INDPRO and PAYEMS decline simultaneously, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) rise above 1,200K units annualized, signaling robust residential investment demand and forward economic expansion. This always-on mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as the market prices in sustained demand growth.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,186K units as of 2020-06-01 indicates recovery from pandemic lows
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of observations when HOUST > 1,200K, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window in housing-strong regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 2.5% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0%, signaling monetary policy lag and real rate compression. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as negative real rates support asset prices.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 258.72 and FEDFUNDS at 0.09% as of 2020-07-01
regime_hint: negative real rate regime with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient between inflation and policy rate is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) contracts quarter-over-quarter (annualized rate < -1%), signaling recession entry. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices earnings downward in response to demand collapse.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 17,205.82B (vintage) vs 19,077.99B (revised) as of 2020-04-01 shows large revision risk in pandemic period
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when GDPC1 contracts > 1% annualized, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window in contraction regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth accelerates above 200K persons month-over-month while unemployment (UNRATE) declines, signaling robust labor market momentum. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as strong employment supports consumer spending and earnings growth.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth > 200K and UNRATE declines > 0.2pp month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 139,582K and UNRATE at 10.2% as of 2020-07-01 reflect post-shock labor market recovery
regime_hint: labor market recovery with accelerating job creation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances despite joint PAYEMS acceleration and UNRATE decline, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 2-year window.
